author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
paratattva vicakṣaṇa, vyāsa⋅ādi munigaṇa,
śāstra vicāriyā bāra bāra
prabhu, tava nityarūpa, guṇaśīla anurūpa,
tomāra caritra sudhāsāra

śuddhasattvamayī līlā, mukhyaśāstre prakāśilā,
jīvera kuśala suvidhāne
rajastamoguṇa⋅andha, asura⋅prakṛti manda,
jane tāhā bujhite nā jāne

nāhi māne nityarūpa, bhajiyā maṇḍbandhakūpa,
rahe tāhe udāsīnaprāya
e bhaktivinoda gāya, ki durdaiva hāya hāya,
haridāsa hari nāhi pāya
en_translation:
O Hari! Sages like Vyāsa and others, experts in the Supreme Truth, repeatedly deliberate the scriptures. O Lord, Your eternal form, with its corresponding divine qualities and characteristics, and Your pastimes are the essence of nectar.

These pastimes consisting of pure goodness are revealed in the prominent scriptures for the ultimate welfare of the souls. But those blinded by the modes of passion and ignorance, with a demoniac nature, are unable to understand this.

They do not accept the eternal form, and resorting to the dark well of material existence, they remain almost indifferent. Bhaktivinoda sings: Alas, what great misfortune! A servant of Hari does not attain Hari!
